name: Las Hurdes Villages Loop
name_original: Villanueva de la Sierra desde Pinofranqueado – circular por Casar de Palomero
description: This moderate 30.6-mile road cycling route in Las Hurdes offers picturesque views and climbs, taking about 2 hours and 25 minutes.
region_intro: Road cycling around Pinofranqueado traverses the rugged landscape of Las Hurdes, characterized by its deep valleys, winding rivers, and dense woodlands. The region presents a challenging terrain with significant elevation changes, offering routes that climb through hills and descend into secluded villages. Road surfaces vary, providing a mix of experiences for cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Pinofranqueado The most popular road cycling route is Las Hurdes Villages Loop, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route navigates through several traditional villages, offering a glimpse into the local architecture and culture. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Esperabán Valley Trail, a moderate 33.3 miles (53.6 km) path. This route features sustained climbs and descents through the scenic Esperabán Valley, with views of the surrounding hills and forests. Local road cyclists also love the Las Hurdes, El Portillo – Portillo Viewpoint loop from Caminomorisco, a 79.4 miles (127.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and to elevated viewpoints, often completed in about 7 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Pinofranqueado is defined by its challenging climbs, scenic valleys, and remote village connections. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, with 16 routes in each category. The routes in Pinofranqueado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 1 review. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pinofranqueado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Pinofranqueado?
A: Road cycling in Pinofranqueado offers a challenging experience due to its rugged landscape and significant elevation changes. The routes are primarily categorized as moderate or difficult, with 16 routes in each category. Expect sustained climbs and descents through valleys and hills.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Pinofranqueado?
A: Pinofranqueado's road cycling routes are generally geared towards moderate to difficult levels, featuring considerable elevation changes. While there are no routes specifically designated as 'easy' or 'family-friendly' in the traditional sense, experienced families or those with older children might find some of the moderate routes manageable, keeping in mind the challenging terrain.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes around Pinofranqueado?
A: The routes traverse the unique landscape of Las Hurdes, characterized by deep valleys, winding rivers, and dense woodlands. You'll encounter traditional villages, offering glimpses into local architecture and culture, along with scenic views of surrounding hills and forests. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057584505/esperaban-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esperabán Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt; showcase these natural vistas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available in Pinofranqueado?
A: Yes, several routes in Pinofranqueado are designed as loops. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057623224/las-hurdes-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Hurdes Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 49.3 km circular trail that navigates through traditional villages. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40306958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Hurdes, El Portillo – Portillo Viewpoint loop from Caminomorisco&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 127.8 km through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: As you cycle, you might encounter various natural and cultural highlights. The region is known for its mountain passes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6967106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esperabán Pass&lt;/a&gt;, offering elevated viewpoints. You can also find natural pools and picnic areas, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area, Shelter, and Natural Pool El Cuadro&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a break.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Pinofranqueado?
A: Given the region's rugged terrain and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn are generally ideal for road cycling in Pinofranqueado. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, making the significant climbs and descents more enjoyable. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder conditions to the higher elevations.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take to complete in Pinofranqueado?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's distance and elevation. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057623224/las-hurdes-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Hurdes Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 25 minutes, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40306958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Hurdes, El Portillo – Portillo Viewpoint loop from Caminomorisco&lt;/a&gt; can take about 7 hours 8 minutes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Pinofranqueado?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the scenic beauty of the valleys, and the opportunity to connect with remote villages, offering a unique and immersive experience in the Las Hurdes region.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly good viewpoints?
A: Yes, routes that ascend to higher elevations often provide stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40306958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Hurdes, El Portillo – Portillo Viewpoint loop from Caminomorisco&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to lead to elevated viewpoints, offering expansive vistas of the mountainous terrain.

Q: What are the distances of road cycling routes in Pinofranqueado?
A: Road cycling routes in Pinofranqueado vary widely in length. You can find moderate routes around 50 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057623224/las-hurdes-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Hurdes Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; (49.3 km) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057584505/esperaban-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esperabán Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt; (53.6 km). For longer, more challenging rides, options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40306958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Hurdes, El Portillo – Portillo Viewpoint loop from Caminomorisco&lt;/a&gt; extend to 127.8 km.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there in Pinofranqueado?
A: There are 32 road cycling routes available around Pinofranqueado, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are evenly split between moderate and difficult classifications.
tips: Nice town in the heart of Las Hurdes

Villanueva de La Sierra is a small town in the Sierra de Gata region. The town's claim to fame is that over 200 years ago, the townspeople decided to celebrate the "Fiesta del Arbol", planting trees in celebration with delicious local food and wine.

Torrecilla de los Ángeles is a small town located on the EX-204 road between Pinofranqueado and Villanueva de La Sierra in the Sierra de Gata region. There is a very nice view of the city and the valley landscape from the Ermita de los Santitus up above, if you like to ride your bike uphill.
Pinofranqueado is a small town located in the beautiful region of Las Hurdes through which the Los Angeles River passes. You can swim on a hot day on the river beach.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Pinofranqueado, so you can typically find parking within the town. As it's a circular route, you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a rolling to hilly landscape with various climbs and descents. The surfaces are generally well-paved and suitable for road bikes, but be prepared for elevation changes throughout the ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and points of interest can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers picturesque views through the Sierra de Gata region. You'll pass through charming towns like Casar de Palomero and can explore Villanueva de la Sierra Historical Landmarks. A highlight is the View of Torrecilla de los Ángeles from the Ermita de los Santitus, offering expansive vistas. You'll also be near the Río Los Ángeles River Beach, Pinofranqueado.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, it's generally not recommended for dogs to accompany you, especially given the traffic and speeds involved. If you bring a dog, they should be kept on a leash in towns and public areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gata region, where this route is located, can get quite warm in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer more pleasant temperatures for cycling, with less extreme heat and beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this route in Las Hurdes?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route. It follows public roads through the region of Las Hurdes.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near towns like Pinofranqueado, Villanueva de la Sierra, and Casar de Palomero. These villages typically offer local cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options where you can refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the CIMA CC05, Camino del Ribero, Camino de Ahigal, and Calle Buenavista during your ride.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and elevation changes, you should pack appropriate cycling gear, including a helmet, water bottles, snacks, a repair kit, and sun protection. Layers are always a good idea, especially during shoulder seasons.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists or families?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' due to its distance and significant elevation gain. While the roads are well-paved, it requires good fitness and is generally more suited for experienced road cyclists rather than beginners or young families.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning you will start and finish in the same location, Pinofranqueado, without having to retrace your path.
